Index.title: Monitor Your Website's Global Performance
Index.subtitle: Test uptime, latency, and DNS resolution from 20+ locations worldwide. Fast, free, and reliable network monitoring.

Metadata.pingTitle: Ping Online - Check Website Connectivity Worldwide
Metadata.pingDesc: Test ICMP ping to any host or IP from multiple global locations. Check latency and packet loss in real-time.
Metadata.httpTitle: HTTP Check - Monitor Website Uptime & Performance
Metadata.httpDesc: Verify HTTP status and response times from 20+ global locations. Fast, reliable uptime monitoring for any website.
Metadata.dnsTitle: DNS Lookup - Check DNS Records Worldwide
Metadata.dnsDesc: Verify global DNS propagation. Check A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, and TXT records across multiple nameservers instantly.
Metadata.ipinfoTitle: IP Info & Geolocation - Detailed Network Intelligence
Metadata.ipinfoDesc: Find detailed geolocation, ISP, and network data for any IP address or domain. Multiple provider comparison.
Metadata.checksTitle: Diagnostic Tools - Global Website Performance Monitoring
Metadata.checksDesc: Comprehensive website diagnostic tools including Ping, HTTP, DNS, SSL, and MTR checks. Multimodal analysis from 20+ locations.
Metadata.smtpTitle: SMTP Test - Check Mail Server Connection & Security
Metadata.smtpDesc: Verify SMTP server connectivity and response from 20+ locations. Test ports 25, 465, 587 and check mail server availability worldwide.
Metadata.sslTitle: SSL Certificate Checker - Validate TLS Security & Expiry
Metadata.sslDesc: Check SSL/TLS certificate validity, expiry date, chain of trust and cipher strength from multiple global locations. Free and instant.

CookieConsent.title: We use cookies
CookieConsent.description: We use essential and analytics cookies to ensure the platform runs correctly and to help us understand usage patterns.

Legal.privacyIntro: Welcome to {siteName} ({siteUrl}). We are committed to protecting your personal information and your right to privacy. This document explains which data we collect, why we collect it, and how we safeguard it.
Legal.autoCollect3: Pages visited and time spent on pages
Legal.userProvided2: Hosts and domains you submit for monitoring checks
Legal.cookiesDesc: We use the following categories of cookies:
Legal.cookieEssentialDesc: Required for authentication and core platform functionality. Cannot be disabled.
Legal.cookieAnalyticsDesc: Help us understand how users interact with the platform so we can improve performance and usability.
Legal.cookieAdvertisingDesc: Store interface preferences such as theme selection and language settings.
Legal.cookiesGoogleNote: We do not use advertising or tracking cookies from third-party ad networks.
Legal.howWeUse1: To provide, operate, and maintain our diagnostic monitoring services
Legal.howWeUse2: To analyze usage patterns and continuously improve our tools
Legal.howWeUse3: To ensure platform security and detect abuse
Legal.howWeUse4: To respond to support requests and technical inquiries
Legal.thirdPartyAdsense: Cloudflare — used for DDoS protection, CDN, and performance optimization.
Legal.thirdPartyCloudflare: Cloudflare — used for DDoS protection and performance optimization.
Legal.thirdPartyAnalytics: Analytics provider — used to collect anonymized, aggregated usage statistics.
Legal.dataRetentionDesc: Diagnostic check logs are retained for a maximum of 90 days. Account data is retained for as long as the account remains active, or until deletion is requested.
Legal.yourRightsDesc: Subject to applicable law, you have the following rights with respect to your personal data:
Legal.right1: Right of access — obtain a copy of the data we hold about you
Legal.right2: Right to rectification — request correction of inaccurate data
Legal.right3: Right to erasure — request deletion of your data
Legal.right4: Right to withdraw consent — at any time, without affecting prior processing
Legal.childrenDesc: This service is not intended for individuals under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors.
Legal.privacyContactDesc: For privacy-related enquiries, please contact us at: {email}
Legal.termsAcceptanceDesc: By accessing or using {siteName} ({siteUrl}), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please discontinue use of the platform.
Legal.serviceDescText: {siteName} is a professional network diagnostic and monitoring platform that provides:
Legal.serviceFeature1: Global Ping, HTTP, DNS, TCP/UDP, and MTR connectivity checks
Legal.serviceFeature2: IP geolocation, ASN, and WHOIS lookup tools
Legal.serviceFeature3: SSL/TLS certificate validity and chain verification
Legal.serviceFeature4: Shareable check snapshots and historical result logs
Legal.acceptableUseDesc: You agree NOT to use this service for:
Legal.prohibited1: Conducting DDoS attacks, stress-tests, or network floods against any target without explicit authorization
Legal.prohibited2: Scanning for security vulnerabilities or performing unauthorized penetration testing
Legal.prohibited3: Any activity that violates applicable local, national, or international law or regulation
Legal.prohibited4: Automated scraping or API usage beyond documented rate limits
Legal.disclaimerDesc: {siteName} is provided 'as is' and 'as available', without warranty of any kind. We do not warrant the accuracy, completeness, or reliability of check results.
Legal.limitationLiabilityDesc: To the fullest extent permitted by law, {siteName} shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising out of or in connection with the use or inability to use the service.
Legal.intellectualPropertyDesc: All content, trademarks, and software on {siteName} are the property of their respective owners. Unauthorised reproduction or redistribution is prohibited.
Legal.terminationDesc: {siteName} reserves the right to suspend or permanently terminate access to the service for any user who violates these Terms of Service or engages in harmful conduct.
Legal.governingLawDesc: These Terms of Service are governed by applicable international law. Disputes shall be resolved in good faith; where necessary, the parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of a competent court.
Legal.changesDesc: {siteName} may update these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated via the platform. Continued use after publication of updates constitutes acceptance.
Legal.termsContactDesc: For terms-related enquiries, contact us at: {email}
Legal.contactSubtitle: Have a question or need assistance? Our team is ready to help.
Legal.generalSupportDesc: For questions about platform features, diagnostic results, or account management.
Legal.abuseReportsDesc: To report misuse, suspected abuse, or a security vulnerability.
Legal.responseTimeDesc: We aim to respond to all enquiries within 24–48 business hours.

header.titleLine2: from Every Corner of the Globe

globalCoverage.desc: 20+ monitoring nodes across Europe, Americas, and Asia for accurate worldwide results.

shareableReports.title: Shareable Reports

tools.title: All Diagnostic Tools at a Glance

dns.name: DNS
dns.measures: A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, TXT records

whatIsCheckNode.a: CheckNode is a free, professional-grade network monitoring platform. It lets you run Ping, HTTP, DNS, MTR, TCP, UDP, and IP diagnostics from 20+ global nodes instantly — right from your browser.

whoIsItFor.a: Developers debugging server latency, webmasters verifying uptime, sysadmins diagnosing routing anomalies, and anyone who needs accurate network data from multiple locations simultaneously.

whyUseCheckNode.a: Unlike single-location tools, CheckNode checks from many regions at once. This reveals CDN issues, regional outages, and DNS propagation differences that local checks cannot detect.

HomePageSeoBlock.footerText: Whether you are debugging a slow server response, verifying DNS propagation after a domain migration, or checking worldwide accessibility — CheckNode provides the precision tools you need, entirely for free.

ping.title: What is a Ping Test and How Does It Work?
ping.understandingDesc: A Ping test is an essential network diagnostic tool used to test the reachability of a host on an IP network. It operates by sending ICMP Echo Request packets to the target and waiting for a reply.
ping.latencyDesc: The round-trip time (RTT) for packets is known as latency, measured in milliseconds (ms). High latency causes slow website loading and poor application performance.

latencyList.fast: Fast Response (0-50ms): Excellent connection, ideal for real-time applications.
latencyList.acceptable: Acceptable Response (50-150ms): Good connection, suitable for general web browsing.
latencyList.slow: Slow Response (>150ms): Noticeable delay that impacts interactive applications.

ping.globalDesc: Testing from a single location only shows part of the picture. By using a global ping tool, you verify routing efficiency and identify regional network outages simultaneously.

http.title: HTTP Status Check: Verify Website Availability
http.understandingDesc: An HTTP check simulates a real browser sending a request to your web server. It verifies if your website is online, responds with the correct HTTP status code (like 200 OK), and measures exactly how long it takes to fetch the page content.
http.latencyDesc: Website speed is critical for user experience and SEO. A slow Time to First Byte (TTFB) or long DNS resolution time can drive visitors away.

latencyList.fast: Fast (0-500ms): Excellent server response and optimized network path.
latencyList.acceptable: Average (500-1500ms): Typical response time, but can be improved with caching.
latencyList.slow: Slow (>1500ms): Poor user experience, indicating server overload or network issues.

http.globalDesc: Your server in Frankfurt might load instantly for European users, but time out for visitors in Sydney. Global HTTP checks reveal CDN misconfigurations, regional blocking, and latency bottlenecks.

dns.title: Global DNS Propagation Checker
dns.understandingDesc: The Domain Name System (DNS) translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses. A DNS Lookup queries authoritative nameservers to fetch specific records (A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, TXT) associated with your domain.
dns.latencyDesc: Different records serve specific functional purposes in your network architecture:

latencyList.a: A & AAAA: Maps a domain name to an IPv4 (A) or IPv6 (AAAA) address.
latencyList.mx: MX (Mail Exchange): Directs email to the correct mail server.
latencyList.txt: TXT: Holds text information, crucial for email security (SPF, DKIM, DMARC).

dns.globalDesc: When you change your domain's hosting, the new IP address must propagate across global DNS caches. This tool checks multiple resolvers worldwide to ensure your updates are visible everywhere.

mtr.title: MTR (Multi-Traceroute): Advanced Network Diagnostics
mtr.understandingDesc: MTR combines the functionality of the 'traceroute' and 'ping' programs in a single network diagnostic tool. It investigates the network connection between the host MTR runs on and a user-specified destination host.
mtr.latencyDesc: By continuously sending packets to each router (hop) along the path, MTR provides real-time statistics about network health.

latencyList.loss: Packet Loss (%): High loss at a specific hop often indicates a congested router or faulty link.
latencyList.ping: Latency (Ping): Sudden spikes in response time reveal where network slowdowns originate.
latencyList.routing: Routing Path: Identify if traffic is taking an optimal geographical route.

mtr.globalDesc: When users report connectivity issues, MTR helps pinpoint exactly where the connection fails—whether it's at your ISP, a backbone provider, or the destination data center.

tcpUdp.title: TCP and UDP Port Availability Check
tcpUdp.understandingDesc: Port checks determine the status of specific network ports. TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) establishes a reliable connection, while UDP (User Datagram Protocol) sends data without a preliminary connection.
tcpUdp.latencyDesc: Verifying port availability is essential for system administrators configuring firewalls and deploying new services.
tcpUdp.globalDesc: Testing ports globally ensures your services aren't being inadvertently blocked by regional ISP firewalls or geoblocking rules configured on your server.

ipInfo.title: IP Address Information & Geolocation
ipInfo.understandingDesc: IP Info tools extract detailed metadata associated with a specific IP address or domain name. This includes geographic coordinates, the governing Internet Service Provider (ISP), and the Autonomous System Number (ASN).
ipInfo.latencyDesc: Understanding who owns an IP address and where it is physically located is crucial for numerous administrative tasks:

latencyList.security: Security: Investigate suspicious traffic or failed login attempts.
latencyList.routing: Routing: Verify BGP routing and CDN edge node resolving.
latencyList.compliance: Compliance: Ensure data is hosted in the correct geographic jurisdiction.

ipInfo.globalDesc: In addition to location data, advanced lookups provide administrative contact information (WHOIS) for an IP block, which is necessary for reporting network abuse or spam.

ssl.title: SSL/TLS Certificate Checker
ssl.understanding: What Does an SSL Certificate Check Verify?
ssl.understandingDesc: An SSL/TLS check analyzes the security certificate of a website, confirming it is valid, trusted by major browsers, and not expired. It reveals the certificate authority (CA), expiry date, and encryption strength.
ssl.latencyDesc: An expired or misconfigured SSL certificate causes browser security warnings, blocking visitors immediately. Proactive monitoring prevents unexpected outages.

latencyList.expiry: Certificate Expiry: Certificates have a validity period. An expired cert immediately breaks HTTPS for all visitors.
latencyList.chain: Chain of Trust: A broken chain (missing intermediate certificate) causes untrusted warnings on some browsers.
latencyList.cipher: Cipher Suite: Weak encryption algorithms (like RC4 or MD5) expose users to man-in-the-middle attacks.

ssl.globalDesc: Different regions may resolve to different servers due to CDN or GeoDNS configurations. A global SSL check confirms that all edge servers present a valid certificate, not just your local resolver.

smtp.title: SMTP Mail Server Connectivity Test
smtp.understandingDesc: An SMTP check verifies if your mail server is correctly accepting connections from external networks. It follows the standard SMTP communication protocol (EHLO/HELO) to ensure the server responds appropriately.
smtp.latencyDesc: Mail server delays can cause email delivery failures or significant delays in message processing. Monitoring response speed is crucial for reliable communication.

latencyList.connection: Connection Time: Measures how long it takes to establish a TCP handshake with the mail server.
latencyList.banner: SMTP Banner: The initial response from the server identifying itself. Missing or slow banners can indicate overload.
latencyList.auth: Security/Ports: Verifying accessibility via standard ports like 25 (SMTP), 587 (Submission), or 465 (SMTPS).

smtp.globalDesc: Firewall misconfigurations or regional ISP blocks often prevent mail servers from receiving legitimate emails. A global SMTP test ensures your mail server is reachable from all parts of the world.

Docs.title: Documentation
Docs.subtitle: Guides, API reference, and technical documentation for CheckNode.

About.heroSubtitle: CheckNode provides real-time, unfiltered network intelligence from over 20 global locations, helping developers and system administrators keep the internet fast and reliable.
About.missionP1: The internet is the backbone of modern society, but diagnosing connectivity issues often requires complex setups or expensive enterprise software.
About.missionP2: We built {siteName} to change that. We believe that high-quality, accurate network diagnostic tools should be accessible to everyone—from indie developers launching their first VPS to enterprise site reliability engineers monitoring global infrastructure.
missionItems: No registration required for core tools
Unfiltered ICMP and TCP routing data
About.infraSubtitle: To provide accurate perspectives on latency and routing, we operate a carefully curated network of testing nodes hosted in premium datacenters around the world.
About.principle1Desc: Network issues don't wait, and neither should you. Our minimalist UI combined with highly optimized background queues ensures you get MTR and Ping results in milliseconds.
About.principle2Desc: When you're diagnosing an outage, the tool you use must be the most reliable link in the chain. We build with redundancy at every layer of our stack.
About.principle3Desc: Data without context is noise. Instead of just showing raw logs, we are integrating AI and smart visualizers to help you understand what the network data actually means.
